reviews.author: Rob
reviews.text: Trung Trang Cave is an interesting and easy to visit cave with well lit paths and interesting rock formations. The walk through the cave is short but enjoyable, and the natural atmosphere makes it a nice stop when exploring Cat Ba Island. Worth a quick visit. Careful who has mobility issues as you will need to crouch down several times

reviews.author: rhea barry
reviews.text: Really cool cave definitely worth visiting if your in cat ba, the ticket also includes the walk to the peak/ national pk entrance down the road so it was so worth doing both in the same day- also don’t miss the specimens that are on the second floor in the national park they where very cool and interesting to see

reviews.author: Håkon
reviews.text: Its easily accessible with concrete flooring all the way through, as well as stairs up and down to the openings. The concrete and "ease of access" ruins the feel of it some, but its worth seeing if you havnt been in a karst cave before, but dont like large scale caving

reviews.author: Yuval Cohen
reviews.text: Nice and quick trip. Around 100 steps to climb up at the start and few steps up down along the way. 15-30 min from beginning to end.

reviews.author: G T
reviews.text: This was worth a visit in that it didn't feel like your typical large cave with lots ot stalagmites and stalictites. The entrance is accessed after walking up an external staircase that has great views. You then enter a reasonably spacious cave area. However the route through the caves leads through some quite small passages, some you'll have to crouch to get through, so maybe not best for the very claustrophobic.

reviews.author: Sara
reviews.text: It was a really great hike. And the cave is really big and gives a great Ecco for singing, I wish I had brought my guitar but I don't think it would have liked the hike.

reviews.author: isabella cheng
reviews.text: Visited this cave as part of our 3 day 2 night cruise. We have visited many other caves in Vietnam. This one is relatively beat up by tourists and on the small side in comparison. Our tour guide actually hit the hollow stalagmites to make music! There were writings /cravings on the walls. In short, don't go out of the way to visit if you have more time in Vietnam to check out other caves. Tbh, to visit this cave as part of our cruise, it involved a boat and bus transfer. As someone that traveled thru Vietnam and have seen many other caves, it was not worth my time to visit. If you are going to visit Phong Nha, Ninh Binh or even Danang, there are a lot of caves that are easily 1000% better.

reviews.author: John Bullock
reviews.text: Very good for younger more mobile people, lots of low overheads and bending, nice experience though. Part of day trip from Hanoi, long day, 3 stops, including silk and pearl stores. Cruise also part of package. Was a good day out.

reviews.author: Jenny Droppa
reviews.text: Entrance is free if you've been to the National Park. The caves are worth seeing, they are well lit and walkways are good. You can enjoy a meal and drinks at the restaurant on exiting the caves.

reviews.author: Michu Schwindl (Daomonk)
reviews.text: 220 Meter before being the restaurant is the exit without check. If you missed you can still go from there to the cave for free. I just meet one and he missed the day before. Ticket is 120k for cave and the sightseeing walk some km fare. 160k for also the village but there you need the whole day he said.

reviews.author: Bella Godfrey
reviews.text: This place was amazing! The alleged history of the trung Trang sisters made this a must stop for me, and it felt so powerful and eerie to walk where they once walked. I just wish the history was more evident there, because it seemed like everyone else was just there to see a big cave, but they didn’t know the legend of the land and how historic this spot was to cat ba

reviews.author: Lester Yee
reviews.text: Interesting. Paid 120k/person for visit to this cave and entrance to Cat Ba national park. Path is generally well litted. Finish the walk in less than 30 mins.

reviews.author: prajwal kulkarni
reviews.text: If you’ve visited any other cave anywhere else, this can be easily skipped. Felt nothing special or different about this cave. If you’re buying tickets for the catba national park, the ticket usually includes entry for trung Trang caves too totalling to 120K VND per person, but if you’ve visited any other caves, you can omit tickets for the caves and get the tickets for 80K VND instead.

reviews.author: Mark Weymouth
reviews.text: A pleasant little excursion. The steps in are metal, and most of the path is concrete, so its doable in flip-flops, though trainers / sneakers woukd be better.

reviews.author: trompoly studio
reviews.text: It was a nice hike and walk through the cave. Super humid but it was fun walking through with my friends. We went with Mon Cherie Cruise and they were pretty good with their services.

reviews.author: Derek Hardwick
reviews.text: All I can say is Wow! I've been in a few caves around Australia from Tassy to WA, and I have seen nothing like this Cave. Very mystical, surrounded by lush green foliage. I avoided the crowds and used a taxi arranged by my hotel Dung Lai Hotel 173. Spent a good part of 45m-1hr in the cave itself. It has many steps, some sections of path where if you are over 5'11 and not flexible your going to have problems, as there are low ceilings and squeeze points. If claustrophobic I would not recommend.

reviews.author: Pat and Erika
reviews.text: Very beautiful cave and a decent size too. Not far from the road but quite a few stairs if for someone with mobility issues.

reviews.author: Maxell Richardson
reviews.text: Smallish cave in a cool location. Well lit and easily accessed by able body visitors. Not a ton of info about the cave on sight but if you do a quick bit of research in advance you will understand the cool limestone caves and formations here.

reviews.author: Milan Marecek
reviews.text: You can go troght the cave by yourself and avoid tourist groups. Approx 500m long and interesting.

reviews.author: kevin learmonth
reviews.text: Taxi dropped me at Trung Trang cave. Ticket was 120k. Young guy at ticket office spoke very good English. He said it takes about 15-20 minutes easy walk. I walked about 100 metres down path then right up stairs to cave entrance. Well lit cave with good path inside. Narrow in one place, turn sideways to get through. Also some low areas, where you will be squatting down or on your hands and knees, depending on how good your knees are. Easy walk. Nice rock formations inside. At the end you will see some Bats on the ceiling of the cave chilling out. There's two types of Bats in the area. You then walk the exit path. There's a cafe near end of the path near the road. Beers and drinks for sale. My taxi driver was waiting for me there and we continued onto the Hospital cave a few minutes away. Not a big cave, but a good little walk.

reviews.author: chu xuan
reviews.text: You can buy a ticket from the Cat Ba National Park to also include this cave for 120,000 dong. The cave is amazing. Pure silence inside, the serene sound of water dripping, beautiful cave formations. Just enough light to paint shadows on the textured walls. Unfortunately I didn't stay long inside enough to fully admire everything :( but would definitely go again! And apparently you can park your motorcycle for free near the exit (you will exit a different path the entrance).

reviews.author: Manolis Lekkas
reviews.text: Worth visiting, if nearby. Look at the photos to get an idea of the images you’d encounter. Everything is regular in size (140 stairs to reach the entrance, 300m the cave length) so it’s the ideal start of the morning for those cruising the area. In April the right gear to wear is shorts & sneakers.

reviews.author: Vaidas Vilemaitis
reviews.text: A beautiful and easily accessible cave in the Cat Ba National Park area. The same ticket grants access to both the park and the cave. The cave is a short, easy walk of about 10-15 minutes, with stunning stalactites and interesting passages. It’s well worth exploring if you’re nearby. Since you’re already here, don’t miss hiking in the National Park—it offers amazing trails and scenic views that complement the cave visit perfectly!

reviews.author: Mia M
reviews.text: Beautiful cave self guided but be careful if you are super tall or over weight there are some tight squeezes

reviews.author: Hashim Fakhreddin
reviews.text: I'm not an expert when it comes to caves, I have visited a few and this one was quite underwhelming. If you come with a group it'll be pretty packed, you'll all move in a single line and not much time to explore, look around or take photos. Pretty quick walk through. If it's raining bring a rain coat and waterproof shoes. Overall a pleasant experience but not something that is a must see.

reviews.author: Ronnie Scott Innes
reviews.text: Pretty cool, well lit at a better price than other caves on cat ba

reviews.author: Vic
reviews.text: Trung trang Cave was a great visit! Tickets: we had the cave for free because we went to the national park. If you do both, it's 120k vnd. The cave is surprisingly long and stunning. I would not recommend to enter if you are claustrophobic as you stay inside a good 15mn. Inside the cave is beautiful. I'd definitely recommend to enter. Parking is free.

reviews.author: Phil Ingles
reviews.text: An excursion from off our ship to the caves. Boat tender, then bus to entrance, then its steps to the entrance, halfway up the mountain. Nice and cool inside, with an easy walk down thru the cave system. A number of good photo spots along the way. Exit caves, away from the entrance. Caves are lit up and modified in some spots to make the going easier.

reviews.author: Audrée
reviews.text: Beautiful cave, bigger than expected Just has some stairs to the entrance of the cave so watch out if you’re scared of heights Ps: price includes for the national park

reviews.author: Joshua Macey
reviews.text: A long and beautiful cave well worth the visit, took some time to walk through! No information boards, but unless this is your first cave you should understand what it all is and means. Very quiet, basically the only people there which made it feel more special. Just something to appreciate on a beautiful island. The ticket includes the national park, so definitely worth the price of 80kd, we did the hike after! The drive is beautiful too.
